info.title: pre.dev Architect API
info.description: Generate comprehensive software specifications for coding agents. The Architect API helps you create detailed project specifications that AI coding agents can understand and implement.

post.description: Generate a comprehensive project specification quickly. Perfect for MVPs and prototypes with balanced depth and speed. Cost: Variable (~5-10 credits based on complexity). Processing time: ~30-40 seconds.

value.input: Build a SaaS project management tool with team collaboration, real-time updates, task tracking, and time logging
Add a calendar view and Gantt chart visualization
value.currentContext: Existing task management system with list and board views, user auth, and basic team features

value.input: Build a comprehensive e-commerce platform with inventory management

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

429.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded

post.description: Generate an ultra-detailed, enterprise-grade project specification. Best for complex systems, enterprise applications, and critical projects. Cost: Variable (~10-50 credits based on complexity). Processing time: ~2-3 minutes. Requires Solo Premium or Enterprise subscription.

value.input: Build an enterprise healthcare management platform with patient records, appointment scheduling, billing, insurance processing, and HIPAA compliance for a multi-location hospital system
Add AI-powered diagnostics, predictive analytics, and automated treatment recommendations
value.currentContext: Existing platform has patient management, scheduling, basic reporting, built with React/Node.js/PostgreSQL, serves 50+ medical practices

value.input: Build a comprehensive fintech platform with banking, investments, crypto trading, regulatory compliance, and real-time market data

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

example.message: Deep Spec requires Solo Premium or Enterprise subscription

429.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded

get.description: Check the status of an asynchronous specification processing request. Use this endpoint to poll for completion when you make an async request (async: true).

parameters.name: specId
parameters.description: The unique ID returned from an async spec generation request

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

404.description: Not Found - Spec ID not found

get.description: List all specifications with pagination and filtering. Perfect for displaying user spec history, recent specs, or filtered views. This endpoint is free and does not consume credits.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Number of records to skip for pagination

specs.input: Build a SaaS project management tool with team collaboration

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description: Search for specifications using regex patterns. Perfect for finding specs by keywords, patterns, or complex search criteria. Case-insensitive search. This endpoint is free and does not consume credits.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Number of records to skip for pagination

specs.input: Build a payment processing system with Stripe integration

400.description: Bad Request - Missing required query parameter

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description: Get the remaining prototype credits balance for the authenticated API key holder. This endpoint is free and does not consume credits.

400.description: Bad Request - Unable to determine user or organization from API key

example.message: Unable to determine user or organization from API key

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

post.summary: Run one or more browser tasks
post.description:
Run one or more browser-agent tasks. Tasks run in parallel (configurable concurrency). Default behavior is synchronous — the request waits for all tasks to complete and returns the full batch result. Set `async: true` to return immediately with a `batchId` you can poll. Set `stream: true` to receive Server-Sent Events (per-step events + final results).

Max 1000 tasks per request. Max 1000 in-flight tasks per user. Subscribed users get 100 requests/minute.

200.description: Batch result (sync mode) or batch stub (async mode).

schema.description: SSE frames: `event: task_event | task_result | done | error`

400.description: Missing or invalid `tasks` array, `tasks.length > 1000`, or a task without a `url`.

429.description: Per-user in-flight queue depth exceeded (max 5000). Retry later or reduce concurrency.

503.description: SSE capacity exceeded on this pod (5000 concurrent streams). Retry with `async: true` and poll.

get.description: Fetch a single run by id. Works for both in-progress and completed runs. Pending tasks are surfaced as stubs in `results` with `status: "PENDING"`. Pass `includeEvents=true` to include the full per-step event timeline for each task plus a top-level `liveEvents` array for tasks that are still running.

parameters.name: includeEvents
parameters.description: Include per-step event timelines. Payloads can be large — screenshots inflate them fast.

404.description: Run not found, or not owned by this API key.

get.description: Paginated list of batches this API key has run.

200.description: Paginated list of runs owned by this API key.

batches.description: Run summaries (field is named `batches` for backwards compatibility).

apiKeyAuth.description: API key for authentication. Get your API key from https://pre.dev/projects/playground (Solo) or https://pre.dev/enterprise/dashboard?page=api (Enterprise). Use format: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY

input.description: Description of what you want to build or the feature you want to add
input.example: Build a SaaS project management tool with team collaboration and real-time updates

currentContext.description: CRITICAL: Existing project/codebase context. When provided, generates feature addition spec. When omitted, generates full new project spec with setup, deployment, docs, maintenance
currentContext.example: Existing Next.js app with Supabase, has auth, task CRUD, team features

docURLs.description: Optional array of documentation URLs that Architect will reference when generating specifications. Each URL is automatically scraped and packaged into downloadable ZIP archives organized by platform

async.description: If true, returns immediately with requestId for status polling. If false (default), waits for completion

file.description: Optional file to be parsed as input (e.g., existing code, documentation, requirements). Can be used alone or combined with input text

input.description: Optional additional text description when using file upload. Can be empty string if using only file

docURLs.description: JSON stringified array of documentation URLs

humanSpecUrl.description: URL where the human-readable spec is hosted (downloadable markdown)

totalHumanHours.description: Estimated total hours for a human to implement the spec

architectureInfographicUrl.description: URL to a visual architecture infographic/diagram for the specification

codingAgentSpecUrl.description: URL where the coding agent spec format is hosted (downloadable markdown)

codingAgentSpecJson.description: Structured JSON spec optimized for AI coding assistants (excludes hours, personas, roles)

codingAgentSpecMarkdown.description: Markdown spec optimized for AI coding assistants

humanSpecJson.description: Full structured JSON spec with hours, personas, and roles for human review

humanSpecMarkdown.description: Full markdown spec with all details for human review

predevUrl.description: pre.dev project URL where you can view and edit the spec

zippedDocsUrls.description: Array of scraped documentation archives. Empty array if no docURLs provided or scraping fails. Each object contains platform identifier and download links

creditsUsed.description: Total credits consumed by this spec generation. Available in real-time during processing and persisted on completion. Typical values: Fast spec ~5-10, Deep spec ~10-50.

userFlowGraph.description: User flow graph with nodes representing user stories/flows and edges showing navigation paths (only when completed)

architectureGraph.description: System architecture graph with C1/C2 level nodes and their relationships (only when completed)

enrichedTechStack.description: Enriched tech stack with detailed reasons, descriptions, and alternatives for each technology (only when completed)

specId.description: Unique ID to poll for status (use with /api/spec-status/:specId)

_id.description: MongoDB ObjectId of the spec request

created.description: ISO timestamp when the request was created

uploadedFileShortUrl.description: Short URL for uploaded file (if file was uploaded)

uploadedFileName.description: Name of uploaded file (if file was uploaded)

humanSpecUrl.description: URL where the human-readable spec is hosted (only when completed)

totalHumanHours.description: Estimated total hours for a human to implement the spec (only when completed)

architectureInfographicUrl.description: URL to a visual architecture infographic/diagram for the specification (only when completed)

codingAgentSpecUrl.description: URL where the coding agent spec format is hosted (only when completed)

codingAgentSpecJson.description: Structured JSON spec optimized for AI coding assistants (only when completed)

codingAgentSpecMarkdown.description: Markdown spec optimized for AI coding assistants (only when completed)

humanSpecJson.description: Full structured JSON spec with hours, personas, and roles (only when completed)

humanSpecMarkdown.description: Full markdown spec with all details for human review (only when completed)

executionTime.description: Processing time in milliseconds (only when completed or failed)

zippedDocsUrls.description: Array of scraped documentation archives (only when completed). Empty array if no docURLs provided or scraping fails

creditsUsed.description: Total credits consumed by this spec generation. Available in real-time during processing and persisted on completion. Typical values: Fast spec ~5-10, Deep spec ~10-50.

userFlowGraph.description: User flow graph with nodes representing user stories/flows and edges showing navigation paths (only when completed)

architectureGraph.description: System architecture graph with C1/C2 level nodes and their relationships (only when completed)

enrichedTechStack.description: Enriched tech stack with detailed reasons, descriptions, and alternatives for each technology (only when completed)

message.example: You have insufficient credits to generate a spec. Please purchase more credits or upgrade your plan.

specs.description: Array of spec objects matching the filters

total.description: Total number of specs matching the filters

platform.description: Hostname extracted from the documentation URL (e.g., 'stripe.com', 'docs.github.com')

masterZipShortUrl.description: Short URL to download the zipped documentation archive for this platform

masterMarkdownShortUrl.description: Optional short URL to consolidated markdown file for this platform

category.description: Category of the technology (e.g., Frontend, Backend, Database)

name.description: Full name of the role (e.g., Full Stack Developer)

shortHand.description: Short abbreviation for the role (e.g., FSD)

subTasks.description: List of subtasks for this story

stories.description: List of user stories in this milestone

CodingAgentSpecJson.description: Simplified structured JSON for AI coding tools (excludes hours, personas, roles)

subTasks.description: List of subtasks for this story

stories.description: List of user stories in this milestone

HumanSpecJson.description: Full structured JSON with hours, personas, and roles for human review

totalHours.description: Total estimated hours for the project

creditsRemaining.description: The number of remaining credits available for the user

type.description: Node type category. For architectureGraph: "frontend", "api-services", "databases", or "external-services". For userFlowGraph: "flow", "role", etc.

level.description: Graph depth level (1, 2, 3...) for userFlowGraph nodes. Not present on architectureGraph nodes.

name.description: Name of the technology (e.g., React, PostgreSQL)

reason.description: Why this technology was chosen for this specific project

description.description: Technical description of what the technology does

helpfulLinks.description: Array of helpful documentation and tutorial links

alternatives.description: Array of alternative technologies that could be used instead

url.description: Starting page URL the agent navigates to.

instruction.description: Natural-language goal. What should the agent accomplish on this page?

input.description: Form values, credentials, queries — any string inputs the agent should use during the task. Keys referenced from `instruction` (e.g. "Search for {{query}}") are interpolated before the run.

output.description: JSON Schema describing the shape of data to extract. When present, the agent's final output is validated against this schema before returning `SUCCESS`. If omitted, the agent returns unstructured text in `data`.

successCondition.description: Optional natural-language assertion. Task is marked `SUCCESS` only if this condition holds at the end (e.g. "the page shows a confirmation with an order number").

timeoutMs.description: Per-task max runtime in milliseconds. Default 240000 (4 min). When reached, the task status becomes `TIMEOUT` and is not billed.

concurrency.description: How many tasks to run in parallel. Default: 5.

async.description: If true, returns `{ id, status: 'processing' }` immediately — poll `GET /:id` for progress.

stream.description: If true, returns an SSE stream with `task_event`, `task_result`, `done`, and `error` frames.

status.description: Only `SUCCESS` is billed; every failure mode is free.

data.description: Extracted data, validated against the task's `output` schema. `null` when no `output` was specified or the task failed.

creditsUsed.description: Credits billed for this task. 1 credit = $0.10. Floor 0.1 for SUCCESS, scales up with task complexity. Zero for non-SUCCESS statuses.

error.description: Failure reason, populated when `status` is not `SUCCESS`.

events.description: Full per-step event timeline. Only present when the caller requested `includeEvents=true`.

RunnerEvent.description: One step in the agent's execution. Internal fields (sandbox provider, LLM model, token counts) are stripped before the event is returned.

data.description: Event-specific payload. See the Get a Run docs for per-type field lists.

BatchResult.description: Run summary. The schema is named `BatchResult` for backwards compatibility with older clients.

completed.description: Number of tasks that have finished (any status).

results.description: Per-task results aligned by `taskIndex`. In-progress runs return PENDING stubs for tasks that haven't started; the stub has only `url`, `instruction`, `input`, and `status: "PENDING"`.

totalCreditsUsed.description: Sum of credits billed across all tasks in the run.

liveEvents.description: Only present when `includeEvents=true` on `GET /:id`. Per-task in-flight event streams for tasks that haven't yet finished. Aligned by index with `results`; completed tasks get an empty array.
